meta_title: Impact Windows in Boca Del Mar, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Boca Del Mar, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & meet Palm Beach County code. Free quotes!
h1: Impact Windows for Boca Del Mar Homes — Strength, Savings, and Peace of Mind
intro:
Boca Del Mar is one of Palm Beach County's most established and desirable residential communities — a place where tree-lined streets, well-kept homes, and a strong sense of neighborhood pride define everyday life. From the quiet cul-de-sacs of Whisper Walk to the golf course fairways of Boca Lago, homeowners here have invested significantly in their properties, with average home values hovering around $470,000. Protecting that investment against South Florida's unpredictable hurricane season isn't optional — it's essential. Impact windows represent one of the smartest, most enduring upgrades a Boca Del Mar homeowner can make.

Although Boca Del Mar sits approximately six miles inland from the Atlantic coastline, that distance offers less protection than many residents assume. Palm Beach County falls within a 150 mph wind zone, and storms don't weaken meaningfully over that short stretch of terrain. When hurricanes push onshore, the communities of Boca Del Mar, Whisper Walk, and Boca Lago face the same wind-driven rain, airborne debris, and pressure fluctuations that coastal neighborhoods endure. Older homes — and roughly 40% of structures here were built before 1994 — were constructed under outdated building codes that simply weren't designed to withstand the storm intensities we've seen in recent decades.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to homeowners throughout Boca Del Mar and the broader Palm Beach County region. As "The Kings of Service," we specialize exclusively in impact-rated products installed by our fully in-house team — no subcontractors, no shortcuts. Whether you're replacing aging single-pane windows in a Boca Lago villa or doing a whole-home upgrade in Whisper Walk, our process is streamlined, code-compliant, and built around your schedule. The result is a home that's quieter, more energy-efficient, safer during storm season, and more attractive to buyers if you ever choose to sell.

section_why.h2: Why Boca Del Mar Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing the right impact window contractor in Boca Del Mar is just as important as choosing the right product. Many companies in South Florida rely on subcontracted labor — rotating crews with variable experience and limited accountability. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates differently. Every installation we perform is handled by our own trained, licensed, and insured technicians. That means consistent quality, direct communication, and a team that stands fully behind its work on every project in Boca Del Mar and across Palm Beach County.

With over 500 homes completed across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our team has seen every type of home configuration, window opening, and structural challenge that South Florida construction presents. Boca Del Mar's housing stock is particularly varied — ranging from early 1980s concrete block ranch homes to more recent two-story Mediterranean-style residences. Our installers understand how to work within each of these construction types, ensuring proper sealing, anchoring, and flashing that goes beyond minimum code and into genuine long-term performance. We pull all required permits through the Palm Beach County Building Division and manage the inspection process from start to finish.

Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau reflects a company culture that prioritizes transparency and communication. We provide detailed written estimates, explain every product option clearly, and never pressure homeowners into decisions that don't fit their needs or budget. For Boca Del Mar residents who've spent years building equity in their homes, we understand that a major upgrade like impact windows deserves a contractor who treats the project — and the home — with real respect.

Financing is also a key part of our mission to make impact protection accessible. We offer options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, meaning Boca Del Mar homeowners don't have to choose between protecting their family and managing monthly cash flow. The combination of professional-grade products, expert in-house installation, and flexible financing is what sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from the competition in this market.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Demands Serious Protection in Boca Del Mar
section_history.body:
Boca Del Mar and the surrounding communities have experienced seven named storms since 1990 — a frequency that underscores just how real the hurricane threat is for Palm Beach County residents. The most formative of these events was Hurricane Andrew in 1992, which struck South Florida as a Category 5 storm and caused catastrophic destruction that reshaped how the entire state thinks about building codes and storm preparedness. While Andrew's most severe damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade County, the storm's reach extended well into Palm Beach County, exposing the vulnerabilities of homes built without modern impact resistance standards. That event directly triggered the sweeping building code revisions of 1994 — revisions that roughly 40% of Boca Del Mar's housing stock predates.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was a more personal reminder for Boca Del Mar residents. Wilma tracked directly across South Florida from west to east, producing widespread wind damage throughout Palm Beach County. The storm's forward speed and intensity caught many homeowners off guard, and properties without impact-rated windows suffered broken glass, water intrusion, and interior damage that took months to remediate. More than a decade later, Hurricane Irma in 2017 brought sustained tropical storm and hurricane-force winds to the region, again highlighting how even storms that don't make direct landfall nearby can cause significant structural stress to older windows and doors.

The cumulative lesson from these storms is clear: standard windows — even relatively modern ones without laminated impact glass — are not adequate protection for homes in this wind zone. For homeowners in Whisper Walk, Boca Lago, and throughout the Boca Del Mar community, upgrading to fully rated impact windows is the single most effective step you can take to protect your home, your family, and your belongings during the next major weather event.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Boca Del Mar falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), which mandates that all new window installations and replacements in this area meet hurricane-resistance standards appropriate for a 150 mph wind zone. While Boca Del Mar is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — Palm Beach County still requires impact-rated products that carry valid Florida Product Approvals (FPA). This means windows must be tested and certified to withstand the wind loads, impact resistance, and cyclic pressure requirements defined by the current Florida Building Code.

All window replacement projects in Boca Del Mar require a permit issued through the Palm Beach County Building Division. This process involves submitting product approval documentation, installation drawings, and contractor licensing information before work begins. After installation, a county inspector must approve the work before it is considered code-compliant and legally closed out. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire permitting and inspection process on behalf of our clients — we handle the paperwork, coordinate the inspection scheduling, and ensure every installation meets or exceeds what the county requires.

For homeowners in pre-1994 homes — a significant portion of Boca Del Mar's residential inventory — code compliance is especially important. Homes built before the post-Andrew code revisions often have window openings that weren't engineered for modern impact units, requiring additional attention to rough opening dimensions, anchoring methods, and waterproofing details. Our in-house team is experienced with these older construction scenarios and ensures every installation achieves full compliance while also maximizing the long-term performance and energy efficiency of the finished product.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Products Built for Palm Beach County Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier impact window lines in Boca Del Mar: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product families are engineered specifically for Florida's demanding climate conditions and carry the Florida Product Approvals required for installation in Palm Beach County's 150 mph wind zone. These are not off-the-shelf hardware store products — they are purpose-built impact systems designed to perform under the most extreme weather conditions South Florida can produce.

ESW impact windows feature heavy-duty multi-chamber extruded aluminum frames that provide exceptional structural rigidity without sacrificing a clean, modern appearance. The laminated impact glass used in ESW units incorporates a tough interlayer bonded between two panes of glass — this construction ensures that even if the glass surface is struck by debris, the pane holds together rather than shattering inward. ESW windows are also available with Low-E (low-emissivity) glass coatings that significantly reduce solar heat gain, helping Boca Del Mar homeowners lower cooling costs during the long South Florida summer. For a community where homes average nearly $470,000 in value, ESW products deliver a level of performance and aesthetic quality that complements the neighborhood's character.

CWI impact windows offer a complementary option with similarly robust engineering and a range of frame profiles and glass configurations to suit different architectural styles and budget targets. Whether your Boca Del Mar home has a traditional Mediterranean facade, a contemporary open floor plan, or a classic ranch layout, CWI's product flexibility allows for a cohesive, attractive finished result. Both ESW and CWI products provide meaningful noise reduction — a benefit residents near busy corridors like Palmetto Park Road or Military Trail will particularly appreciate — as well as UV protection that preserves interior furnishings, flooring, and artwork over time.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost in Boca Del Mar — and How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
For Boca Del Mar homeowners with properties valued around $470,000, a full impact window replacement project typically represents an investment in the range of $10,000 to $25,000 or more depending on the number of openings, window sizes, chosen product line, and any structural or framing work required. Larger two-story homes with numerous oversized openings — common in Boca Lago and parts of Whisper Walk — will fall toward the higher end of that range, while more modestly sized ranch-style homes may come in significantly lower. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, detailed in-home estimates so Boca Del Mar homeowners always know exactly what they're paying for before any commitment is made.

Financing transforms this investment from a budget challenge into an accessible monthly expense. Our financing options start as low as $87 per month with $0 down, giving homeowners a path to storm protection that doesn't require depleting savings or waiting for the next storm season to pass. When you factor in the insurance premium savings, reduced energy bills, and the added resale value impact windows bring to a Palm Beach County home, the net financial impact over a five-to-ten-year horizon is strongly positive.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact windows consistently rank among the highest-value home improvement projects in South Florida. Real estate professionals throughout the Boca Raton and Delray Beach markets — both neighbors to Boca Del Mar — regularly note that impact-protected homes sell faster and at higher prices than comparable properties without this upgrade. Add in the annual insurance savings (which can range from hundreds to over a thousand dollars per year depending on your carrier and current coverage), and the case for investing in ESW or CWI impact windows in Boca Del Mar becomes financially compelling even before you consider the irreplaceable value of protecting your family.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Real Insurance Savings with Impact Windows in Palm Beach County
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact windows in Boca Del Mar is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida insurance carriers are required by state law to offer discounts for wind mitigation features, and impact-rated windows are among the most highly credited improvements a homeowner can make. After your installation is complete, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can certify your home's upgraded openings, and that report is submitted to your insurance carrier to trigger the appropriate credits — a process Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can help walk you through.

The savings vary by insurer, coverage level, and the specific construction features of your home, but many Boca Del Mar homeowners see annual premium reductions ranging from $500 to $1,500 or more after a full impact window upgrade. For homes in the 33433 ZIP code that carry the elevated premiums now common in the Palm Beach County insurance market — where carriers have tightened underwriting standards dramatically following years of storm losses statewide — these discounts represent meaningful, recurring annual savings that accumulate significantly over the life of the windows.

Major carriers operating in Palm Beach County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several private market insurers, all recognize wind mitigation credits tied to impact window installations. Beyond the premium discount, having fully impact-rated openings can also improve your insurability overall — some carriers have moved toward non-renewing policies on homes without adequate wind protection, making the upgrade not just financially smart but potentially necessary for maintaining continuous coverage. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installations are fully documented with product approval numbers and inspection records, giving you everything your insurance agent needs to apply for maximum available credits.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows if I live in Boca Del Mar, which is several miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Yes — absolutely. Boca Del Mar sits in a 150 mph wind zone under Palm Beach County's building code, and storm systems don't weaken meaningfully over the six miles between the coast and your neighborhood. Hurricanes Andrew, Wilma, and Irma all produced damaging wind speeds throughout this area. Distance from the water reduces storm surge risk, but it provides very limited protection from high winds and flying debris, which are the primary threats impact windows are designed to resist.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Boca Del Mar home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical Boca Del Mar home, a full impact window replacement ranges from approximately $10,000 to $25,000 depending on the number of openings, window sizes, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making the upgrade accessible without a large upfront payment. We provide free, no-obligation in-home estimates so you know exactly what your specific project will cost before making any decisions.

faqs.q: What permits are required to install impact windows in Boca Del Mar, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: All impact window installations in Boca Del Mar require a permit issued through the Palm Beach County Building Division. The permit process involves submitting Florida Product Approval documentation, installation plans, and contractor credentials. After installation, a county inspection is required to close the permit. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting and inspection process on your behalf — from application submission through final approval — so you don't have to navigate the county bureaucracy on your own.

faqs.q: How long does a typical impact window installation take for a home in Boca Del Mar?
faqs.a: For most Boca Del Mar homes, the physical installation is completed in one to three days depending on the number of windows, the complexity of the openings, and any structural considerations. The permitting process with Palm Beach County Building Division typically adds two to four weeks prior to the installation start date. Our team coordinates scheduling efficiently and works to minimize disruption to your daily routine. From signed contract to final inspection, most projects are completed within four to six weeks.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance after installing impact windows in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier, coverage level, and home construction, but many Boca Del Mar homeowners see annual premium reductions of $500 to $1,500 or more after a full impact window installation. After your project is complete, a wind mitigation inspection certifies your upgrades, and that report is submitted to your insurer to apply the applicable credits. Florida law requires carriers to recognize these discounts. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's fully documented installations give you everything needed to maximize your available savings.
